name: PostgreSQL Observability
author: OpsDB Platform
description: Monitor PostgreSQL 17 databases: availability, sessions, blocking, replication, WAL, vacuum, indexes, security, query performance, EXPLAIN plan analysis, alert event tracking, and metric drill-down panels.

setup_requirements.title: PostgreSQL Port Reachable
setup_requirements.description: The OpsDB backend must be able to reach the target host on the configured port (default 5432) over TCP. Check firewalls and security groups between the backend and the database host.
fix_commands: -- also confirm any cloud security group / NSG allows inbound TCP 5432 from the OpsDB backend's IP

setup_requirements.title: listen_addresses Allows Remote Connections
setup_requirements.description: postgresql.conf's listen_addresses must include the backend's network interface (not just 'localhost') or the server won't accept remote connections at all.
fix_commands: -- then restart PostgreSQL for the change to take effect

setup_requirements.title: pg_hba.conf Allows This Host, User, and Database
setup_requirements.description: pg_hba.conf needs a matching entry for the connecting host's IP range, database, and username with an accepted auth method — otherwise PostgreSQL rejects the connection before it even checks the password.
fix_commands: -- Add a line to pg_hba.conf (adjust IP range, db, and user):

setup_requirements.title: Valid Username & Password
setup_requirements.description: The username and password stored as a Credential in OpsDB must be valid for this database and role.

setup_requirements.title: SSL Enabled on Server
setup_requirements.description: Only needed when SSL Mode is set to 'require' below. The server must have ssl = on in postgresql.conf with a valid certificate, or the connection will be rejected.

setup_requirements.title: track_io_timing Enabled
setup_requirements.description: Optional, but without it the I/O Stats collector (pg_stat_io) still runs and reports read/write counts — it just can't report read/write timing in ms, so I/O latency findings lose their signal.

setup_requirements.title: pg_stat_statements Extension
setup_requirements.description: Optional, but the Query Performance collectors (Top Queries, Slow Queries, Temp Spills) need it. Without it, those collectors return a friendly 'extension is not installed' message instead of data — everything else still works.

setup_requirements.title: pg_monitor Role Granted
setup_requirements.description: Optional, but if the connecting user isn't a superuser, granting the built-in pg_monitor role lets it see all sessions, statements, and I/O stats instance-wide instead of just its own — otherwise several Activity/Performance collectors under-report.

pg17_availability.description: Server version number, standby status (0=primary,1=standby), and uptime in seconds.
pg17_availability.query: SELECT current_setting('server_version_num')::int AS server_version_num, pg_is_in_recovery()::int AS is_standby, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - pg_postmaster_start_time()))::numeric AS uptime_seconds

pg17_activity_sessions.description: Session state counts (total/active/idle/idle-in-transaction/waiting), connection usage percentage, and time-based risk metrics: longest active query, longest idle-in-transaction, and longest wait in seconds.
pg17_activity_sessions.query: WITH s AS ( SELECT setting::int AS max_connections FROM pg_settings WHERE name = 'max_connections'), agg AS ( SELECT   count(*)                                                            AS total_sessions,   count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'active')                           AS active_sessions,   count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'idle')                             AS idle_sessions,   count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'idle in transaction')              AS idle_in_transaction_sessions,   count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'idle in transaction (aborted)')    AS idle_in_transaction_aborted_sessions,   count(*) FILTER (WHERE wait_event_type IS NOT NULL)                AS waiting_sessions,   round(count(*)::numeric * 100.0 / NULLIF((SELECT max_connections FROM s), 0), 1)                                                                       AS connection_usage_pct,   COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - query_start)     FILTER (WHERE state = 'active'))::numeric, 0)                    AS max_active_query_seconds,   COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - xact_start)     FILTER (WHERE state = 'idle in transaction'))::numeric, 0)       AS max_idle_xact_seconds,   COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - query_start)     FILTER (WHERE wait_event_type IS NOT NULL))::numeric, 0)         AS max_wait_seconds FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE pid <> pg_backend_pid()) SELECT (SELECT max_connections FROM s) AS max_connections, agg.* FROM agg

pg17_blocking_locks.description: Count of blocked backend processes and the worst blocked duration in seconds.
pg17_blocking_locks.query: SELECT count(*) AS blocking_count, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - blocked.query_start))::numeric, 0) AS max_blocked_duration_seconds FROM pg_stat_activity blocked JOIN pg_locks blocked_locks ON blocked_locks.pid = blocked.pid WHERE NOT blocked_locks.granted

pg17_long_transactions.description: Count of transactions open longer than 15 minutes and the age of the oldest one in seconds.
pg17_long_transactions.query: SELECT count(*) AS long_transaction_count, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - xact_start))::numeric, 0) AS max_transaction_age_seconds FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE xact_start IS NOT NULL AND now() - xact_start > interval '15 minutes' AND pid <> pg_backend_pid()

pg17_database_stats.description: Buffer cache hit percentage, temp file usage, deadlock count, commit/rollback counts for the current database.
pg17_database_stats.query: SELECT blks_read, blks_hit, round(blks_hit::numeric / NULLIF(blks_hit + blks_read, 0) * 100, 2) AS cache_hit_pct, temp_files, temp_bytes, deadlocks, xact_commit, xact_rollback FROM pg_stat_database WHERE datname = current_database()

pg17_io_stats.description: Aggregated I/O read/write counts, timing in ms, evictions, and buffer hits across all I/O backends (requires track_io_timing = on). Uses pg_stat_io (PostgreSQL 16+).
pg17_io_stats.query: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(reads), 0) AS total_reads, COALESCE(SUM(writes), 0) AS total_writes, COALESCE(SUM(read_time), 0) AS total_read_time_ms, COALESCE(SUM(write_time), 0) AS total_write_time_ms, COALESCE(SUM(evictions), 0) AS total_evictions, COALESCE(SUM(hits), 0) AS total_hits FROM pg_stat_io

pg17_wal_stats.description: WAL record count, full-page images, bytes generated, and WAL buffer full count from pg_stat_wal (PostgreSQL 17+).
pg17_wal_stats.query: SELECT wal_records, wal_fpi, wal_bytes, wal_buffers_full FROM pg_stat_wal

pg17_checkpoint_bgwriter.description: Checkpoint counts (timed vs requested), write/sync timing, buffer stats, and percentage of demand-triggered checkpoints. Uses pg_stat_checkpointer + pg_stat_bgwriter (PostgreSQL 17+).
pg17_checkpoint_bgwriter.query: SELECT c.num_timed AS checkpoints_timed, c.num_requested AS checkpoints_requested, c.write_time AS checkpoint_write_time_ms, c.sync_time AS checkpoint_sync_time_ms, c.buffers_written AS buffers_checkpoint, b.buffers_clean, b.maxwritten_clean, b.buffers_alloc, round(c.num_requested::numeric / NULLIF(c.num_timed + c.num_requested, 0) * 100, 2) AS requested_checkpoint_pct FROM pg_stat_checkpointer c, pg_stat_bgwriter b

pg17_physical_replication.description: Count of standbys, streaming standbys, maximum replay lag in bytes and seconds.
pg17_physical_replication.query: SELECT count(*) AS replication_standby_count, count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'streaming') AS replication_streaming_count, COALESCE(max(pg_wal_lsn_diff(sent_lsn, replay_lsn)), 0) AS max_replay_lag_bytes, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(replay_lag))::numeric, 0) AS max_replay_lag_seconds FROM pg_stat_replication

pg17_wal_receiver.description: WAL receiver count and streaming receiver count (standby-side view).
pg17_wal_receiver.query: SELECT count(*) AS wal_receiver_count, count(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'streaming') AS wal_receiver_streaming_count FROM pg_stat_wal_receiver

pg17_replication_slots.description: Total, active, and inactive replication slot counts; maximum retained WAL bytes across all slots.
pg17_replication_slots.query: SELECT count(*) AS total_slots, count(*) FILTER (WHERE active) AS active_slots, count(*) FILTER (WHERE NOT active) AS inactive_slots, COALESCE(max(pg_wal_lsn_diff(pg_current_wal_lsn(), restart_lsn)), 0) AS max_retained_wal_bytes FROM pg_replication_slots

pg17_subscription_status.description: Logical replication subscription count, running subscriptions, and maximum receive lag in bytes.
pg17_subscription_status.query: SELECT count(*) AS subscription_count, count(*) FILTER (WHERE pid IS NOT NULL) AS running_subscriptions, COALESCE(max(pg_wal_lsn_diff(latest_end_lsn, received_lsn)), 0) AS max_receive_lag_bytes FROM pg_stat_subscription

pg17_subscription_table_sync.description: Logical replication subscription table sync status: total subscribed tables, tables in ready state, and tables still synchronising.
pg17_subscription_table_sync.query: SELECT count(*) AS subscribed_tables, count(*) FILTER (WHERE srsubstate = 'r') AS ready_tables, count(*) FILTER (WHERE srsubstate <> 'r') AS syncing_tables FROM pg_subscription_rel

pg17_table_vacuum_pressure.description: Tables with dead tuples, count with dead tuple ratio >= 20%, maximum dead tuple percentage, and total dead tuples.
pg17_table_vacuum_pressure.query: SELECT count(*) AS tables_with_dead_tuples, count(*) FILTER (WHERE round(n_dead_tup::numeric / NULLIF(n_live_tup + n_dead_tup, 0) * 100, 2) >= 20) AS tables_high_dead_pct, COALESCE(max(round(n_dead_tup::numeric / NULLIF(n_live_tup + n_dead_tup, 0) * 100, 2)), 0) AS max_dead_tuple_pct, COALESCE(sum(n_dead_tup), 0) AS total_dead_tuples FROM pg_stat_user_tables WHERE n_live_tup + n_dead_tup > 0

pg17_vacuum_progress.description: Count of actively running VACUUM operations and the maximum heap scan progress percentage.
pg17_vacuum_progress.query: SELECT count(*) AS active_vacuum_count, COALESCE(max(round(heap_blks_scanned::numeric / NULLIF(heap_blks_total, 0) * 100, 2)), 0) AS max_vacuum_progress_pct FROM pg_stat_progress_vacuum

pg17_analyze_progress.description: Count of actively running ANALYZE operations.
pg17_analyze_progress.query: SELECT count(*) AS active_analyze_count FROM pg_stat_progress_analyze

pg17_index_usage.description: Total user indexes, count of unused (zero-scan) indexes, and total wasted space in unused indexes in bytes.
pg17_index_usage.query: SELECT count(*) AS total_user_indexes, count(*) FILTER (WHERE idx_scan = 0) AS unused_indexes, COALESCE(sum(CASE WHEN idx_scan = 0 THEN pg_relation_size(indexrelid) ELSE 0 END), 0) AS unused_index_size_bytes FROM pg_stat_user_indexes

pg17_table_sizes.description: Total user table count, combined size in bytes, and size of the largest single table in bytes.
pg17_table_sizes.query: SELECT count(*) AS total_tables, COALESCE(sum(pg_total_relation_size(c.oid)), 0) AS total_size_bytes, COALESCE(max(pg_total_relation_size(c.oid)), 0) AS largest_table_bytes FROM pg_class c JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace WHERE c.relkind IN ('r', 'p', 'm') AND n.nspname NOT IN ('pg_catalog', 'information_schema')

pg17_database_sizes.description: Current database total size in bytes.

pg17_top_queries_pg_stat_statements.description: Top 25 queries ordered by total cumulative execution time from pg_stat_statements. Best for finding queries that consume the most overall DB time. See pg17_slow_queries_by_mean for queries slowest per individual call.
pg17_top_queries_pg_stat_statements.query: SELECT substring(query, 1, 500) AS query, calls::int AS calls, round(mean_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS query_mean_exec_ms, round(total_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS total_exec_time_ms, rows::int AS total_rows FROM pg_stat_statements ORDER BY total_exec_time DESC LIMIT 25

pg17_query_temp_spills.description: Count of queries with any temp block usage and the maximum temp spill for a single query in MB (requires pg_stat_statements).
pg17_query_temp_spills.query: SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE temp_blks_read + temp_blks_written > 0) AS queries_with_temp_spill, COALESCE(max((temp_blks_read + temp_blks_written) * 8.0 / 1024), 0) AS max_temp_spill_mb FROM pg_stat_statements

pg17_configuration_baseline.description: 27 key GUC parameters: memory sizing, WAL durability, autovacuum, timeouts, security, and observability settings. Includes pending-restart count.
pg17_configuration_baseline.query: SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE pending_restart) AS settings_pending_restart, max(CASE WHEN name='track_counts' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS track_counts, max(CASE WHEN name='track_io_timing' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS track_io_timing, max(CASE WHEN name='autovacuum' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS autovacuum, max(CASE WHEN name='log_checkpoints' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS log_checkpoints, max(CASE WHEN name='log_lock_waits' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS log_lock_waits, max(CASE WHEN name='log_connections' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS log_connections, max(CASE WHEN name='ssl' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS ssl_enabled, max(CASE WHEN name='synchronous_commit' THEN (setting IN ('on','remote_apply','remote_write'))::int END) AS synchronous_commit_safe, max(CASE WHEN name='max_connections' THEN setting::numeric END) AS max_connections, max(CASE WHEN name='max_wal_senders' THEN setting::numeric END) AS max_wal_senders, max(CASE WHEN name='autovacuum_max_workers' THEN setting::numeric END) AS autovacuum_max_workers, max(CASE WHEN name='max_parallel_workers_per_gather' THEN setting::numeric END) AS max_parallel_workers, max(CASE WHEN name='checkpoint_completion_target' THEN setting::numeric END) AS checkpoint_completion_target, max(CASE WHEN name='random_page_cost' THEN setting::numeric END) AS random_page_cost, max(CASE WHEN name='autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor' THEN setting::numeric END) AS autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor, max(CASE WHEN name='log_min_duration_statement' THEN setting::numeric END) AS log_min_duration_statement, max(CASE WHEN name='idle_in_transaction_session_timeout' THEN setting::numeric END) AS idle_in_transaction_session_timeout_ms, max(CASE WHEN name='statement_timeout' THEN setting::numeric END) AS statement_timeout_ms, max(CASE WHEN name='lock_timeout' THEN setting::numeric END) AS lock_timeout_ms, max(CASE WHEN name='shared_buffers' THEN setting::numeric * 8192 END) AS shared_buffers_bytes, max(CASE WHEN name='work_mem' THEN setting::numeric * 1024 END) AS work_mem_bytes, max(CASE WHEN name='maintenance_work_mem' THEN setting::numeric * 1024 END) AS maintenance_work_mem_bytes, max(CASE WHEN name='max_wal_size' THEN setting::numeric * 1048576 END) AS max_wal_size_bytes, max(CASE WHEN name='effective_cache_size' THEN setting::numeric * 8192 END) AS effective_cache_size_bytes, max(CASE WHEN name='shared_preload_libraries' THEN (setting LIKE '%pg_stat_statements%')::int END) AS shared_preload_has_pss, max(CASE WHEN name='wal_level' THEN (setting IN ('replica','logical'))::int END) AS wal_level_ok FROM pg_settings WHERE name IN ('track_counts','track_io_timing','autovacuum','log_checkpoints','log_lock_waits','log_connections','ssl','synchronous_commit','max_connections','max_wal_senders','autovacuum_max_workers','max_parallel_workers_per_gather','checkpoint_completion_target','random_page_cost','autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor','log_min_duration_statement','idle_in_transaction_session_timeout','statement_timeout','lock_timeout','shared_buffers','work_mem','maintenance_work_mem','max_wal_size','effective_cache_size','shared_preload_libraries','wal_level')

pg17_extension_inventory.description: Total number of installed PostgreSQL extensions.
pg17_extension_inventory.query: SELECT count(*) AS installed_extensions_count FROM pg_extension

pg17_role_security.description: Total roles, superuser count, replication-only role count, and expired login role count.
pg17_role_security.query: SELECT count(*) AS total_roles, count(*) FILTER (WHERE rolsuper) AS superuser_count, count(*) FILTER (WHERE rolreplication AND NOT rolsuper) AS replication_only_role_count, count(*) FILTER (WHERE rolcanlogin AND rolvaliduntil < now()) AS expired_login_role_count FROM pg_roles

pg17_database_privileges.description: Total database count and number of non-template user databases.
pg17_database_privileges.query: SELECT count(*) AS total_databases, count(*) FILTER (WHERE NOT datistemplate) AS user_databases_count FROM pg_database

pg17_autovacuum_activity.description: Count of active autovacuum worker processes and age of the longest-running autovacuum in seconds.
pg17_autovacuum_activity.query: SELECT count(*) AS active_autovacuum_count, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - query_start))::numeric, 0) AS max_autovacuum_age_seconds FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE (backend_type ILIKE '%autovacuum%' OR query ILIKE 'autovacuum:%') AND state IS NOT NULL

pg17_prepared_transactions.description: Count of prepared (two-phase commit) transactions and age of the oldest one in seconds.
pg17_prepared_transactions.query: SELECT count(*) AS prepared_transaction_count, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - prepared))::numeric, 0) AS max_prepared_age_seconds FROM pg_prepared_xacts

pg17_invalid_indexes.description: Count of invalid or not-ready indexes and their total wasted disk space in bytes.
pg17_invalid_indexes.query: SELECT count(*) AS invalid_index_count, COALESCE(sum(pg_relation_size(c.oid)), 0) AS invalid_index_total_bytes FROM pg_index i JOIN pg_class c ON c.oid = i.indexrelid WHERE NOT i.indisvalid OR NOT i.indisready

pg17_partition_overview.description: Count of partitioned tables and the maximum number of partitions on a single table.
pg17_partition_overview.query: SELECT count(*) AS partitioned_table_count, COALESCE(max(child_count), 0) AS max_partition_count FROM (SELECT c.oid, count(child.inhrelid) AS child_count FROM pg_class c LEFT JOIN pg_inherits child ON child.inhparent = c.oid WHERE c.relkind = 'p' GROUP BY c.oid) t

pg17_xid_wraparound.description: XID age per database — exceeding 2.1 billion causes PostgreSQL to forcibly shut down to prevent wraparound data corruption. This is the #1 cause of unplanned PG outages.
pg17_xid_wraparound.query: SELECT max(age(datfrozenxid)) AS max_xid_age, count(*) FILTER (WHERE age(datfrozenxid) > 1500000000) AS databases_near_wraparound, count(*) FILTER (WHERE age(datfrozenxid) > 200000000) AS databases_needing_freeze, COALESCE(min(2147483647 - age(datfrozenxid)), 0) AS min_xids_until_wraparound FROM pg_database WHERE datallowconn

pg17_table_activity.description: Table-level DML throughput and scan patterns: total inserts/updates/deletes, sequential vs index scan ratio, HOT update percentage, and large tables being sequentially scanned.
pg17_table_activity.query: SELECT COALESCE(sum(seq_scan), 0) AS total_seq_scans, COALESCE(sum(idx_scan), 0) AS total_idx_scans, COALESCE(round(sum(seq_scan)::numeric / NULLIF(sum(seq_scan) + sum(idx_scan), 0) * 100, 2), 0) AS seq_scan_pct, COALESCE(sum(n_tup_ins), 0) AS total_inserts, COALESCE(sum(n_tup_upd), 0) AS total_updates, COALESCE(sum(n_tup_del), 0) AS total_deletes, COALESCE(sum(n_tup_hot_upd), 0) AS total_hot_updates, COALESCE(round(sum(n_tup_hot_upd)::numeric / NULLIF(sum(n_tup_upd), 0) * 100, 2), 0) AS hot_update_pct, count(*) FILTER (WHERE seq_scan > 100 AND n_live_tup > 10000) AS large_tables_seqscanned FROM pg_stat_user_tables

pg17_wait_events.description: Breakdown of what sessions are waiting for (Lock, IO, LWLock, Client, CPU/running). Includes aggregate wait-type counts in the first row for finding evaluation. THE primary tool for diagnosing why queries are slow.
pg17_wait_events.query: WITH events AS (  SELECT COALESCE(wait_event_type,'CPU') AS wait_event_type,         COALESCE(wait_event,'Running') AS wait_event,         count(*) AS session_count  FROM pg_stat_activity  WHERE pid <> pg_backend_pid() AND state NOT IN ('idle')  GROUP BY 1,2),summary AS (  SELECT    count(*) FILTER (WHERE wait_event_type = 'Lock')   AS lock_wait_sessions,    count(*) FILTER (WHERE wait_event_type = 'IO')     AS io_wait_sessions,    count(*) FILTER (WHERE wait_event_type = 'LWLock') AS lwlock_wait_sessions,    count(*) FILTER (WHERE wait_event_type = 'Client') AS client_wait_sessions  FROM pg_stat_activity  WHERE pid <> pg_backend_pid() AND state NOT IN ('idle') AND wait_event_type IS NOT NULL)SELECT e.wait_event_type, e.wait_event, e.session_count,       s.lock_wait_sessions, s.io_wait_sessions, s.lwlock_wait_sessions, s.client_wait_sessions FROM events e, summary s ORDER BY e.session_count DESC

pg17_active_queries.description: Snapshot of all non-idle sessions: PID, user, application, state, wait event, query age, transaction age, and truncated query text. Use this to see exactly what is running and waiting right now.
pg17_active_queries.query: SELECT pid,       usename,       application_name,       client_addr::text AS client_addr,       state,       COALESCE(wait_event_type,'—') AS wait_event_type,       COALESCE(wait_event,'—') AS wait_event,       round(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - query_start))::numeric, 1) AS query_age_seconds,       round(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - xact_start))::numeric, 1) AS xact_age_seconds,       left(query, 300) AS query_text FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state NOT IN ('idle') AND pid <> pg_backend_pid() ORDER BY query_age_seconds DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 30

pg17_lock_tree.description: Full blocking chain: for each blocked backend shows who is blocking it, on which relation, what type of lock, and both query texts. Drill-down complement to pg17_blocking_locks.
pg17_lock_tree.query: SELECT  blocked.pid                                                    AS blocked_pid,  blocked.usename                                                AS blocked_user,  left(blocked.query, 250)                                       AS blocked_query,  round(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now()-blocked.query_start))::numeric,1) AS blocked_seconds,  blocker.pid                                                    AS blocker_pid,  blocker.usename                                                AS blocker_user,  left(blocker.query, 250)                                       AS blocker_query,  COALESCE(blocked_locks.locktype,'')                            AS lock_type,  COALESCE(blocked_locks.relation::regclass::text,'')            AS locked_relation FROM pg_locks blocked_locks JOIN pg_stat_activity blocked  ON blocked_locks.pid = blocked.pid JOIN pg_locks blocker_locks   ON  blocker_locks.locktype = blocked_locks.locktype   AND blocker_locks.relation IS NOT DISTINCT FROM blocked_locks.relation   AND blocker_locks.granted AND NOT blocked_locks.granted JOIN pg_stat_activity blocker ON blocker_locks.pid = blocker.pid ORDER BY blocked_seconds DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 20

pg17_table_io_hotspots.description: Per-table cache hit ratios from pg_statio_user_tables. First row reports the minimum cache hit percentage across all user tables (min_table_cache_hit_pct) and count of tables below 90% hit rate. Subsequent rows detail each table.
pg17_table_io_hotspots.query: WITH tio AS (  SELECT schemaname, relname,         heap_blks_read, heap_blks_hit,         round(heap_blks_hit::numeric / NULLIF(heap_blks_hit + heap_blks_read, 0) * 100, 1) AS cache_hit_pct,         (heap_blks_read + heap_blks_hit) AS total_heap_access  FROM pg_statio_user_tables  WHERE heap_blks_read + heap_blks_hit > 1000),summary AS (  SELECT round(min(cache_hit_pct), 1)                               AS min_table_cache_hit_pct,         count(*) FILTER (WHERE cache_hit_pct < 90)                  AS tables_below_90pct  FROM tio) SELECT t.schemaname, t.relname, t.cache_hit_pct, t.heap_blks_read, t.heap_blks_hit,        t.total_heap_access, s.min_table_cache_hit_pct, s.tables_below_90pct FROM tio t, summary s ORDER BY t.heap_blks_read DESC LIMIT 30

pg17_seq_scan_hotspots.description: Per-table sequential scan counts and sizes. First row includes count of large tables (>100 MB) with >80% seq scan ratio — prime candidates for missing indexes. Use this to identify which tables need indexes added.
pg17_seq_scan_hotspots.query: WITH scans AS (  SELECT schemaname, relname,         seq_scan, COALESCE(idx_scan, 0) AS idx_scan,         seq_tup_read, n_live_tup,         pg_total_relation_size(quote_ident(schemaname)||'.'||quote_ident(relname)) AS table_bytes,         round(seq_scan::numeric / NULLIF(seq_scan + COALESCE(idx_scan,0), 0) * 100, 1) AS table_seq_scan_pct  FROM pg_stat_user_tables  WHERE seq_scan > 50),summary AS (  SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE table_seq_scan_pct > 80 AND table_bytes > 104857600) AS large_tables_no_index,         max(seq_scan) AS max_seq_scan_count  FROM scans) SELECT s.schemaname, s.relname, s.seq_scan, s.idx_scan,        s.n_live_tup, s.table_seq_scan_pct,        pg_size_pretty(s.table_bytes) AS table_size,        sm.large_tables_no_index, sm.max_seq_scan_count FROM scans s, summary sm ORDER BY s.seq_scan DESC LIMIT 30

pg17_slow_queries_by_mean.description: Top 25 queries ordered by mean execution time (slowest individual queries). Includes stddev for plan instability detection. Requires pg_stat_statements. Complements pg17_top_queries_pg_stat_statements which sorts by total time.
pg17_slow_queries_by_mean.query: SELECT substring(query, 1, 500)                           AS query,       calls::int                                         AS calls,       round(mean_exec_time::numeric, 2)                  AS mean_exec_time_ms,       round(stddev_exec_time::numeric, 2)                AS stddev_exec_time_ms,       round(total_exec_time::numeric, 2)                 AS total_exec_time_ms,       rows::int                                          AS total_rows,       round(shared_blks_read::numeric / NULLIF(shared_blks_hit + shared_blks_read,0)*100,1) AS cache_miss_pct FROM pg_stat_statements WHERE calls > 3 ORDER BY mean_exec_time DESC LIMIT 25

pg17_recent_errors.description: Recent non-idle backend activity including errors, slow queries and problematic states from pg_stat_activity. Acts as a lightweight activity log when PostgreSQL file-based logging is unavailable.
pg17_recent_errors.query: SELECT pid,       usename,       application_name,       client_addr::text AS client_addr,       backend_type,       state,       COALESCE(wait_event_type,'—') AS wait_event_type,       COALESCE(wait_event,'—') AS wait_event,       round(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - query_start))::numeric, 1) AS query_age_seconds,       round(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - state_change))::numeric, 1) AS state_age_seconds,       left(query, 400) AS query_text FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE pid <> pg_backend_pid()   AND (state NOT IN ('idle') OR wait_event_type IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY query_age_seconds DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 100

pg17_log_entries.description: Reads the last 32 KB of the most recently modified PostgreSQL log file. Dynamically resolves the log directory and filename via pg_ls_logdir(). Requires pg_monitor role or superuser (pg_read_file + pg_ls_logdir).
pg17_log_entries.query: WITH latest_log AS ( SELECT   current_setting('log_directory') || '/' || name AS log_file_path,   name                                             AS log_file,   modification                                     AS last_modified,   size::bigint                                     AS size_bytes FROM pg_ls_logdir() WHERE name ~ '\.(log|csv|json)$' ORDER BY modification DESC LIMIT 1) SELECT   log_file,   log_file_path,   last_modified,   pg_size_pretty(size_bytes) AS file_size,   pg_read_file(     log_file_path,     GREATEST(0, size_bytes - 32768),     LEAST(size_bytes, 32768)   ) AS recent_lines FROM latest_log WHERE size_bytes > 0

pg17_connection_usage_warning.title: Connection Usage High (Warning)
pg17_connection_usage_warning.recommendation: Review active and idle connections. Consider connection pooling (PgBouncer) and tuning max_connections.

pg17_connection_usage_critical.title: Connection Usage Critical
pg17_connection_usage_critical.recommendation: Immediate action required: nearly all connections are consumed. Terminate idle connections or enable connection pooling.

pg17_idle_in_transaction_warning.title: Idle-in-Transaction Sessions Detected
pg17_idle_in_transaction_warning.recommendation: Sessions holding open transactions block vacuuming and lock resources. Set idle_in_transaction_session_timeout in postgresql.conf.

pg17_idle_in_transaction_critical.title: High Idle-in-Transaction Session Count
pg17_idle_in_transaction_critical.recommendation: Many sessions are stuck in open transactions. Investigate application connection management and terminate stale backends.

pg17_waiting_sessions_detected.title: Waiting Sessions Detected
pg17_waiting_sessions_detected.recommendation: Multiple sessions are waiting on events or locks. Investigate pg_stat_activity for wait_event details.

pg17_long_active_query_warning.title: Long-Running Active Query (15+ min)
pg17_long_active_query_warning.recommendation: A query has been running for over 15 minutes. Review pg_stat_activity and consider using statement_timeout.

pg17_long_active_query_critical.title: Long-Running Active Query (1+ hour)
pg17_long_active_query_critical.recommendation: A query has been running for over 1 hour. Investigate and consider terminating via pg_terminate_backend().

pg17_blocking_lock_detected.title: Blocking Lock Detected
pg17_blocking_lock_detected.recommendation: At least one backend is blocked waiting for a lock. Identify blocking PIDs in pg_blocking_pids() and evaluate if they should be terminated.

pg17_blocking_lock_prolonged.title: Prolonged Blocking Lock (15+ min)
pg17_blocking_lock_prolonged.recommendation: A backend has been blocked for over 15 minutes. Consider terminating the blocking transaction to restore throughput.

pg17_long_transaction_warning.title: Long Open Transaction (15+ min)
pg17_long_transaction_warning.recommendation: A transaction has been open for over 15 minutes. Long transactions prevent autovacuum from reclaiming dead tuples.

pg17_long_transaction_critical.title: Long Open Transaction (1+ hour)
pg17_long_transaction_critical.recommendation: A transaction has been open for over 1 hour. This can cause table bloat and XID wraparound issues. Investigate and terminate if stale.

pg17_cache_hit_low_warning.title: Cache Hit Rate Below 95%
pg17_cache_hit_low_warning.recommendation: Buffer cache hit rate is low. Consider increasing shared_buffers or reviewing query access patterns.

pg17_cache_hit_low_critical.title: Cache Hit Rate Below 90%
pg17_cache_hit_low_critical.recommendation: Critical: most reads are going to disk. Urgently review shared_buffers, indexes, and query plans.

pg17_deadlocks_detected.title: Deadlocks Detected
pg17_deadlocks_detected.recommendation: Deadlocks indicate conflicting lock acquisition order. Review application transaction patterns and use consistent lock ordering.

pg17_replication_lag_bytes_warning.title: Replication Replay Lag High (1 GB+)
pg17_replication_lag_bytes_warning.recommendation: Standby is more than 1 GB behind the primary. Check network throughput, standby CPU, and replication slot health.

pg17_replication_lag_bytes_critical.title: Replication Replay Lag Critical (5 GB+)
pg17_replication_lag_bytes_critical.recommendation: Standby is more than 5 GB behind. Failover RPO is at serious risk. Investigate immediately.

pg17_replication_lag_seconds_warning.title: Replication Apply Lag High (30+ seconds)
pg17_replication_lag_seconds_warning.recommendation: Standby apply lag exceeds 30 seconds. Review wal_receiver_status_interval and standby workload.

pg17_replication_lag_seconds_critical.title: Replication Apply Lag Critical (5+ minutes)
pg17_replication_lag_seconds_critical.recommendation: Standby apply lag exceeds 5 minutes. DR capability is severely degraded. Investigate standby apply throughput immediately.

pg17_inactive_replication_slot.title: Inactive Replication Slot
pg17_inactive_replication_slot.recommendation: Inactive replication slots prevent WAL cleanup and can cause disk full conditions. Drop unused slots immediately.

pg17_retained_wal_warning.title: Replication Slot Retaining Large WAL (5 GB+)
pg17_retained_wal_warning.recommendation: A replication slot is holding back WAL cleanup. Monitor pg_replication_slots.restart_lsn and consider dropping inactive slots.

pg17_retained_wal_critical.title: Replication Slot Retaining Excessive WAL (10 GB+)
pg17_retained_wal_critical.recommendation: A replication slot is retaining 10 GB+ of WAL. Disk exhaustion risk is high. Drop inactive slots or reduce wal_keep_size.

pg17_checkpoint_ratio_warning.title: Requested Checkpoint Ratio High (20%+)
pg17_checkpoint_ratio_warning.recommendation: More than 20% of checkpoints are demand-triggered. Increase max_wal_size or checkpoint_completion_target.

pg17_checkpoint_ratio_critical.title: Requested Checkpoint Ratio Critical (50%+)
pg17_checkpoint_ratio_critical.recommendation: Over half of all checkpoints are demand-triggered. Significantly increase max_wal_size to reduce write pressure.

pg17_dead_tuple_pct_warning.title: High Dead Tuple Percentage (20%+)
pg17_dead_tuple_pct_warning.recommendation: At least one table has over 20% dead tuples. Check autovacuum settings and long-running transactions.

pg17_dead_tuple_pct_critical.title: Critical Dead Tuple Percentage (40%+)
pg17_dead_tuple_pct_critical.recommendation: A table has over 40% dead tuples causing significant bloat and degraded performance. Run VACUUM ANALYZE immediately.

pg17_old_prepared_transaction_warning.title: Old Prepared Transaction (15+ min)
pg17_old_prepared_transaction_warning.recommendation: A two-phase commit transaction has been prepared for over 15 minutes. This prevents vacuum from cleaning dead tuples.

pg17_old_prepared_transaction_critical.title: Old Prepared Transaction (1+ hour)
pg17_old_prepared_transaction_critical.recommendation: A prepared transaction is over 1 hour old. This can block XID wraparound protection. Roll back or commit via ROLLBACK/COMMIT PREPARED.

pg17_invalid_index_found.title: Invalid Index Detected
pg17_invalid_index_found.recommendation: One or more indexes are invalid (CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY failure or pg_upgrade). Rebuild them with REINDEX CONCURRENTLY.

pg17_superuser_count_high.title: Superuser Account Count High
pg17_superuser_count_high.recommendation: Minimise superuser accounts. Grant only the specific privileges needed. Review role membership in pg_roles.

pg17_settings_pending_restart.title: Configuration Changes Pending Restart
pg17_settings_pending_restart.recommendation: One or more postgresql.conf changes require a server restart to take effect. Plan a maintenance window.

pg17_xid_wraparound_warning.title: XID Wraparound Risk — Age Exceeds 1.5 Billion
pg17_xid_wraparound_warning.recommendation: One or more databases are approaching XID wraparound (limit: ~2.1B). Run VACUUM FREEZE on affected databases immediately and check autovacuum_freeze_max_age. Plan a maintenance VACUUM FREEZE if age > 1.9B.

pg17_xid_wraparound_critical.title: XID Wraparound Imminent — Age Exceeds 2 Billion
pg17_xid_wraparound_critical.recommendation: EMERGENCY: PostgreSQL will force-shutdown at ~2.1B XIDs to prevent data corruption. Run VACUUM FREEZE immediately. If the database is too large, consider pg_upgrade or coordinated freeze. Do not restart without running VACUUM FREEZE first.

pg17_ssl_disabled.title: SSL/TLS Not Enabled
pg17_ssl_disabled.recommendation: Enable SSL by setting ssl = on in postgresql.conf and providing ssl_cert_file / ssl_key_file. All connections should be encrypted in transit. Enforce via pg_hba.conf using hostssl entries.

pg17_no_idle_txn_timeout.title: idle_in_transaction_session_timeout Not Set
pg17_no_idle_txn_timeout.recommendation: Set idle_in_transaction_session_timeout = '60s' to automatically terminate sessions that hold open transactions without activity. Without this, a single stuck client can block autovacuum and cause lock pileups indefinitely.

pg17_synchronous_commit_off.title: synchronous_commit Disabled — Data Loss Risk
pg17_synchronous_commit_off.recommendation: synchronous_commit = off trades durability for throughput. A server crash within ~200ms can lose committed transactions. Set to 'on' for OLTP workloads. If you need the performance, use it only for specific non-critical tables via SET LOCAL.

pg17_high_seq_scan_ratio.title: High Sequential Scan Ratio (50%+)
pg17_high_seq_scan_ratio.recommendation: More than half of all table scans are sequential. Review pg_stat_user_tables for large tables with high seq_scan counts. Add indexes for frequent query predicates. Check random_page_cost vs effective_cache_size if plans look wrong.

pg17_lock_wait_sessions.title: Lock Wait Sessions Detected
pg17_lock_wait_sessions.recommendation: Sessions are actively waiting to acquire locks. Use pg17_lock_tree collector to identify the blocker PID and query. Consider terminating long-running blockers with pg_terminate_backend().

pg17_io_wait_sessions_high.title: High I/O Wait — Sessions Blocked on Disk
pg17_io_wait_sessions_high.recommendation: Multiple sessions are waiting on I/O. Check pg17_table_io_hotspots to identify tables with low cache hit ratios. Consider increasing shared_buffers, enable pg_prewarm, or review query access patterns.

pg17_slow_query_detected.title: Slow Query Detected (Mean > 1 s)
pg17_slow_query_detected.recommendation: At least one query has a mean execution time above 1 second. Review pg17_slow_queries_by_mean. Run EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) on the offending query. Check for missing indexes, bad plan choices, or parameter sniffing.

pg17_slow_query_critical.title: Very Slow Query Detected (Mean > 5 s)
pg17_slow_query_critical.recommendation: A query is averaging over 5 seconds per execution. This is a significant performance issue. Immediately review pg17_slow_queries_by_mean. Run EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS), check for sequential scans on large tables, and verify index coverage.

pg17_table_cache_miss.title: Table-Level Cache Miss Detected (< 85%)
pg17_table_cache_miss.recommendation: One or more tables have a buffer cache hit rate below 85%, meaning frequent disk reads. Review pg17_table_io_hotspots to identify which tables. Consider increasing shared_buffers, using pg_prewarm, or partitioning large hot tables.

pg17_large_table_no_index.title: Large Table with No Index Usage (Sequential Scans Only)
pg17_large_table_no_index.recommendation: One or more tables larger than 100 MB are being accessed almost exclusively via sequential scans. Review pg17_seq_scan_hotspots to identify them. Add appropriate indexes for the predicates used in frequent queries on these tables.

hints: What are the top performance bottlenecks in this database?
Which queries are consuming the most CPU or I/O resources?
Are there any missing indexes causing sequential scans on large tables?
What is the current cache hit ratio and is it acceptable for this workload?
Are there any long-running or blocking transactions that need attention?
How does checkpoint frequency and BGwriter activity look? Are we doing too many forced checkpoints?
Are we approaching the max_connections limit? What is the risk level?
Are there any active locks or deadlocks that should be investigated?
Which sessions are waiting the longest and why?
Is connection pooling recommended based on current connection patterns?
What is the current replication lag and is it within acceptable bounds?
Are there any replication slots at risk of growing unbounded?
What is the overall availability and health status of this instance?
Are standby servers keeping up with the primary's WAL generation rate?
Are there any tables with significant bloat that need VACUUM or ANALYZE urgently?
Are autovacuum settings appropriate for the current table sizes and churn rate?
Which tables or indexes are consuming the most disk space?
Are there any invalid indexes that should be rebuilt?
Are partition tables well-balanced, or are some partitions significantly larger than others?
Are there any security misconfigurations or privilege escalation risks?
Which GUC parameters should be reviewed or tuned for this workload?
Are there any users with excessive or unnecessary privileges?
Are any pg_hba.conf rules overly permissive?
Provide an executive summary of the overall database health.
What are the top 3 most urgent issues to address immediately?
What recommendations do you have for the next 30 days to improve stability and performance?
Based on the findings, what is the overall risk level for this database?

metrics.name: active_sessions
metrics.label: Active Sessions
metrics.hint: Queries currently executing

metrics.name: waiting_sessions
metrics.label: Waiting Sessions
metrics.hint: Sessions waiting on locks or I/O

metrics.name: idle_in_transaction_sessions
metrics.label: Idle-in-Transaction
metrics.hint: Sessions holding open transactions without executing

metrics.name: blocked_sessions
metrics.label: Blocked Sessions
metrics.hint: Sessions blocked by another session holding a lock

metrics.name: long_active_count
metrics.label: Long-Running Queries
metrics.hint: Active queries running longer than 60 s

metrics.name: total_sessions
metrics.label: Total Connections
metrics.hint: All connected clients including background workers

metrics.name: connection_usage_pct
metrics.label: Connection Usage %
metrics.hint: Connections used vs max_connections

metrics.name: cache_hit_pct
metrics.label: Cache Hit Ratio
metrics.hint: Buffer cache hits / (hits + disk reads). Below 95% is a concern.

metrics.name: temp_files
metrics.label: Temp Files
metrics.hint: Temp files created due to sort/hash spills to disk

metrics.name: temp_bytes
metrics.label: Temp File Bytes
metrics.hint: Total bytes written to temp files

metrics.name: seq_scan_pct
metrics.label: Seq Scan %
metrics.hint: Sequential scans as % of all scans; high value = missing indexes

metrics.name: total_seq_scans
metrics.label: Total Seq Scans
metrics.hint: Cumulative sequential scans across all user tables

metrics.name: total_idx_scans
metrics.label: Total Index Scans
metrics.hint: Cumulative index scans across all user tables

metrics.name: hot_update_pct
metrics.label: HOT Update %
metrics.hint: HOT updates as % of total updates; higher = less index bloat

metrics.name: deadlocks
metrics.label: Deadlocks
metrics.hint: Cumulative deadlock count (resets on stats reset)

metrics.name: total_inserts
metrics.label: Total Inserts
metrics.hint: Cumulative rows inserted across all user tables

metrics.name: total_updates
metrics.label: Total Updates
metrics.hint: Cumulative rows updated

metrics.name: total_deletes
metrics.label: Total Deletes
metrics.hint: Cumulative rows deleted

metrics.name: prepared_transaction_count
metrics.label: Prepared Transactions
metrics.hint: Transactions in PREPARED state (2PC); unresolved ones block vacuum

metrics.name: blocking_count
metrics.label: Blocking Count
metrics.hint: Count of blocked backend processes

metrics.name: max_blocked_duration_seconds
metrics.label: Max Blocked Duration
metrics.hint: Age of the longest-blocked session in seconds

metrics.name: lock_wait_sessions
metrics.label: Lock Wait Sessions
metrics.hint: Sessions actively waiting to acquire a lock

metrics.name: tables_with_dead_tuples
metrics.label: Tables With Dead Tuples
metrics.hint: User tables that have any dead (unvacuumed) tuples

metrics.name: tables_high_dead_pct
metrics.label: Tables High Dead %
metrics.hint: Tables with dead tuple ratio >= 20%

metrics.name: max_dead_tuple_pct
metrics.label: Max Dead Tuple %
metrics.hint: Highest dead tuple percentage across all user tables

metrics.name: total_dead_tuples
metrics.label: Total Dead Tuples
metrics.hint: Total dead tuples across all user tables

metrics.name: active_vacuum_count
metrics.label: Active VACUUM Workers
metrics.hint: VACUUM operations currently running

metrics.name: active_autovacuum_count
metrics.label: Autovacuum Workers
metrics.hint: Active autovacuum worker processes right now

metrics.name: max_xid_age
metrics.label: Max XID Age
metrics.hint: Oldest transaction ID age; approaching 2.1B causes emergency shutdown

metrics.name: databases_near_wraparound
metrics.label: DBs Near Wraparound
metrics.hint: Databases with XID age > 1.5B — needs immediate freeze

metrics.name: databases_needing_freeze
metrics.label: DBs Needing Freeze
metrics.hint: Databases with XID age > 200M — should run VACUUM FREEZE soon

metrics.name: min_xids_until_wraparound
metrics.label: XIDs Until Wraparound
metrics.hint: Minimum transactions remaining before wraparound shutdown

metrics.name: max_replay_lag_seconds
metrics.label: Replication Lag
metrics.hint: Physical standby replay lag in seconds

metrics.name: max_replay_lag_bytes
metrics.label: Replay Lag Bytes
metrics.hint: Physical standby replay lag in bytes

metrics.name: wal_bytes
metrics.label: WAL Bytes Generated
metrics.hint: WAL bytes generated (cumulative, from pg_stat_wal)

metrics.name: max_retained_wal_bytes
metrics.label: Max Retained WAL
metrics.hint: WAL bytes retained by the laggiest replication slot

metrics.name: inactive_slots
metrics.label: Inactive Rep Slots
metrics.hint: Replication slots with no active consumer — they prevent WAL cleanup

metrics.name: replication_standby_count
metrics.label: Standby Count
metrics.hint: Connected streaming standbys

metrics.name: requested_checkpoint_pct
metrics.label: Requested Checkpoint %
metrics.hint: % of checkpoints that were demand-triggered (not scheduled)

metrics.name: current_database_size_bytes
metrics.label: Database Size
metrics.hint: Total size of the monitored database

metrics.name: total_size_bytes
metrics.label: Total Table Size
metrics.hint: Combined size of all user tables including indexes

metrics.name: largest_table_bytes
metrics.label: Largest Table
metrics.hint: Size of the single largest table

metrics.name: unused_indexes
metrics.label: Unused Indexes
metrics.hint: Indexes with zero scans since last stats reset

metrics.name: unused_index_size_bytes
metrics.label: Unused Index Size
metrics.hint: Total disk space consumed by unused indexes

metrics.name: invalid_index_count
metrics.label: Invalid Indexes
metrics.hint: Invalid or not-ready indexes — rebuild with REINDEX CONCURRENTLY

metrics.name: mean_exec_time_ms
metrics.label: Max Mean Exec Time
metrics.hint: Highest mean execution time among all queries in pg_stat_statements

metrics.name: queries_with_temp_spill
metrics.label: Queries With Temp Spill
metrics.hint: Queries that wrote to disk during sort/hash operations

metrics.name: max_temp_spill_mb
metrics.label: Max Temp Spill
metrics.hint: Largest single-query temp spill in MB

metrics.name: settings_pending_restart
metrics.label: Pending Restart
metrics.hint: GUC settings changed that require a server restart

metrics.name: ssl_enabled
metrics.label: SSL Enabled
metrics.hint: 1 = on, 0 = off. Trigger at == 0 to alert when SSL is disabled.

metrics.name: synchronous_commit_safe
metrics.label: Synchronous Commit Safe
metrics.hint: 1 = on/remote_apply/remote_write, 0 = off. Trigger at == 0 to alert.

metrics.name: idle_in_transaction_session_timeout_ms
metrics.label: Idle-in-Txn Timeout
metrics.hint: 0 = disabled. Trigger at == 0 to alert when unset.

metrics.name: statement_timeout_ms
metrics.label: Statement Timeout
metrics.hint: 0 = disabled. Trigger at == 0 to alert when unset.

metrics.name: shared_preload_has_pss
metrics.label: pg_stat_statements Loaded
metrics.hint: 1 = loaded, 0 = not. Trigger at == 0 to alert.

metrics.name: uptime_seconds
metrics.label: Server Uptime
metrics.hint: Seconds since PostgreSQL started. Low value may indicate a recent restart.

metrics.name: is_standby
metrics.label: Is Standby
metrics.hint: 1 = standby (replica), 0 = primary

config_templates.query:
SELECT
  datname        AS database,
  numbackends    AS connections,
  xact_commit    AS commits,
  xact_rollback  AS rollbacks,
  blks_hit,
  blks_read,
  tup_returned,
  tup_fetched
FROM pg_stat_database
WHERE datname NOT IN ('template0', 'template1')
ORDER BY numbackends DESC
